approach: Provenance- and target-reference-complement hardening of the fail-closed order-42 control gate

strategy.mechanism: An authoritative-direct provenance and frozen-byte preflight gates two exact evaluators, three complete-adjacency parser paths, byte-exact target nauty complementation, structural controls, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: The target corpus gate can reject non-authoritative provenance and complement-byte disagreement without weakening its existing parser and evaluator controls.
discriminating_test: Run the shared target-gate complement helper on every unlabeled order-7 class and complement, require exact evaluator/parser/reference agreement, and require deliberate complement and provenance mutations to fail.
summary: The target gate now requires authoritative-direct provenance, byte-exact target nauty-complg agreement, and records the compiled Checker B hash. The exhaustive order-7 regression passed on all 1,044 unlabeled classes and complements and rejected complement/provenance mutations. A browser audit could not export the authoritative raw body. The final preflight returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false; zero order-42 records were checked and no Ramsey bound changed.
rationale: The regression closes a concrete correlated-complement and provenance-substitution weakness in the reusable control pipeline. Since no authoritative raw corpus was acquired, this is scoped research-software progress only and the constructive routes remain blocked.
claims: The shared target-gate helper and nauty-complg produced identical complement bytes on all 1,044 unlabeled order-7 graph classes.
A one-bit complement mutation and provenance mutations to authority, byte count, and content type were rejected.
Both exact evaluators and NetworkX retained complete-adjacency agreement on all 2,088 order-7 source/complement records.
No order-42 graph was evaluated; the rigorous range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
next_steps: Acquire unchanged r55_42some.g6 bytes directly from the maintained ANU URL through a genuinely new raw-file path.
Create the authoritative-direct provenance sidecar specified in CHECKPOINT.md without normalizing the raw bytes.
Run the authenticated-corpus command in CHECKPOINT.md and stop on the first provenance, byte, checker, parser, reference-complement, structural, or canonical mismatch.
Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 only after the corpus gate passes.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-214708-5e7b0a: final exhaustive order-7 provenance/complement regression passed
.proof-experiments/20260720-214715-c04ad0: final absent-source preflight returned expected code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false
independent_checker: Checker A uses Python, independently parsed Boolean matrices, and direct 5-subset enumeration. Checker B uses C, a separate graph6 parser, and recursive bitset clique enumeration. NetworkX supplies a third decoder and nauty-complg supplies a reference complement implementation. All ran only on order 7 in this epoch because the order-42 source was absent.
transfer_insights: Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> locally derived complements can share a correlated bug -> add target-instance byte-exact nauty-complg comparison; the shared helper passed the exhaustive order-7 control and rejected a one-bit mutation.

Fail-closed source admission -> matching mirror bytes do not authenticate an authoritative object -> require a raw-byte-linked authoritative-direct provenance sidecar; authority, byte-count, and content-type mutations were rejected.

established_facts.claim: The shared target-gate complement helper agrees byte-for-byte with nauty-complg on every unlabeled order-7 graph class, and a one-bit mutation is detected.
established_facts.scope: 1,044 representatives and 1,044 complements at order 7 only

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
established_facts.evidence: Dynamic Survey DS1.18 and McKay's maintained publication list

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Admit matching mirror bytes as an authenticated McKay corpus without direct-source provenance.
ruled_out.scope: Corpus admission for downstream novelty and repair work
ruled_out.reason: The frozen mirror hash is only an acquisition discriminator and does not prove equivalence to the maintained authoritative object.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Supply authoritative-direct response metadata or explicit maintainer confirmation linking the exact bytes.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start order-42 novelty or K43 repair search from the epoch-4 packet.
ruled_out.scope: Any continuation lacking authenticated McKay and Springer raw artifacts
ruled_out.reason: The final preflight stopped before every target-instance check.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Pass the complete McKay source/control gate and then authenticate the Springer seed.

open_leads.description: Run the provenance- and reference-complement-hardened order-42 gate.
open_leads.rationale: The missing authoritative raw artifact is now the only prerequisite to executing the full supplied-656 control packet.
open_leads.next_experiment: Acquire authoritative bytes and response metadata, create sources/r55_42some.g6.provenance.json as specified in CHECKPOINT.md, and run the recorded command.

open_leads.description: Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 after the corpus passes.
open_leads.rationale: It is the strongest published K43 repair seed, but the current transcription lacks publisher provenance.
open_leads.next_experiment: Retrieve the unchanged TXT, determine its color convention, and require exact violation identities under both checkers.

blocker: The command network cannot resolve source hosts, the browser connector cannot export the raw body, and no authoritative order-42 corpus or provenance sidecar exists locally.
reopen_condition: Provide or retrieve unchanged McKay bytes with authoritative-direct response metadata matching the frozen size, record count, and SHA-256 discriminator.

continuation.objective: Close the authoritative order-42 corpus control gate.
continuation.first_action: Acquire sources/r55_42some.g6 directly from the authoritative URL, create the provenance sidecar specified in CHECKPOINT.md, and run the exact authenticated-corpus command.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop at any provenance, byte, LF termination, record-count, 861-bit parser fingerprint, forbidden-set, histogram, degree, local/reference complement, or canonical-label mismatch.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can future R(5,5) corpus and search claims be gated against provenance substitution and correlated complement-generation errors?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The previous gate lacked target-instance nauty-complg comparison, authoritative provenance validation, and a compiled Checker B hash; these are now enforced or recorded.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: Directly acquired authoritative order-42 bytes pass the complete gate from a cold start and an independent external replay confirms the packet.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this local artifact adds fail-closed provenance and multi-implementation verification and claims no new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey DS1.18, arXiv, and McKay's maintained publication list
novelty_searches.finding: No rigorous update beyond 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46 was located; search non-discovery is not a novelty proof.

external_channel.acceptance_path: No contact or submission until authoritative raw-source replay succeeds.

independent_validations.validator: NetworkX 3.3, nauty-complg, and two local exact checkers
independent_validations.result: Complete adjacency and byte-exact complement agreement on 2,088 order-7 records; four mutation classes rejected

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. Two precomputed GPT-5.6 Terra memos were advisory; the relied experiment-verification memo was hash-verified, promoted byte-for-byte, and independently implemented and mutation-tested by Sol. No new subagent was spawned. Deterministic tools: Python 3.12.3, C/GCC, nauty, NetworkX 3.3, SHA-256, Git diagnostics, the computational-researcher experiment harness, and web browsing. No SAT solver, proof assistant, cloud job, or command-network download was used.
